summary: glob-helper postinstall harvests credentials, browser wallet vaults, and seed phrases to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a multi-stage installer-side credential and crypto-wallet stealer. (1) It reads ~/.npmrc, ~/.env, and ~/.git-credentials, bundles them with os.hostname()/os.userInfo(), and POSTs them as JSON to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at http://149.28.127.35:8888 over plain HTTP (postinstall.js line 7: `const C2=process.env.C2_URL||'http://149.28.127.35:8888'`). (2) scanWallets() enumerates 71 known crypto wallet extension IDs (MetaMask, Phantom, Coinbase, Trust, Ledger, etc.) across Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium/Vivaldi/Opera profile directories, reads each wallet's `Local Extension Settings` LevelDB.log files, and regex-extracts `vault`, `seed`, `mnemonic`, `privateKey`, and `password` fields. (3) scanSeedPhrases() walks ~/Documents, ~/Desktop, and ~/Downloads, opens files matching crypto-related keywords, counts BIP39 wordlist matches, and ships matching file paths plus content previews to the same C2. The package's index.js falsely advertises itself as a 'Lodash JavaScript utilities bundle' to lure installers, while a comment in postinstall.js openly self-describes as 'Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner... Silent. Zero trace.' Every installer of this package has their npm tokens,.env secrets, git credentials, browser wallet vaults, and any local seed-phrase backups exfiltrated to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
